entries.text: Hello, I'm Peter Park, working as a chaplain at Eminent Hospice.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: The first time I started hospice work was in 2012.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I started working in the US hospice for the first time.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I learned about the US hospice system at that time.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I met various patients.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I realized that hospice work is very precious and important.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I think it's been about 10 years already.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I'm not working alone in hospice.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I work as a team. I mainly work as a doctor, nurse, social worker, chaplain, and other nurses.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I work as a team.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I think chaplain can be divided into two main roles.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: One is the SCS, future counselor, and BC.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: There are two areas. As you know, there are many patients who work in hospice.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: Usually, when there is a physical pain, the life quality is improved a lot.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: Likewise, as if there is a physical pain, many people live with a spiritual pain in their heart.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I think there are a lot of people who can't think of it as important.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: If you get control through counseling, the life quality is improved a lot.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I think many people who have died in hospice are suffering more.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: Especially, the hospice chaplain service is very precious.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: It is important to visit and discuss with those who are involved in the service.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: Through that, you can get rid of the pain in your heart.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I think it is very important to share with those who have died in hospice.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: Through counseling, you can get rid of the pain in your heart.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: These are the services we receive before we die in hospice.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: After the patients die, there are many families who love them.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: For those families, through the appointment counseling, you can get rid of the pain in your heart.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: After the patients die, you can discuss with them what kind of services they need.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: There is a service that we have sent to the families for a year.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: How can we get rid of the pain in the family?
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: We are also sharing with the family how to deal with the patient's personal and social problems.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: We are also sharing with the family how to deal with the patient's personal and social problems.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I remember the patients who had died in hospice.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: One of them tried to commit suicide while taking care of the patient.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: It was a person who tried to stop his life because of the pain,
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: but if he had received a little more hospice service,
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: he would have thought that his life would have changed if he had received more hospice service.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: The other person was a person who had died in January of this year.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: He said that he loved his wife.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: He was a person who couldn't say that he loved her so easily.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: After he left, I discussed with my son in the middle of the conversation.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: He said that he loved his father.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: As soon as I heard the story, I felt a great burden on this matter.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: The other person is a person who has been taking care of the patient.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: He is a very active person.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: He doesn't have anything. He thinks everything is perfect.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: A few weeks ago, he told me that he has something to say to his wife.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: Suddenly, his face suddenly changed and he started to cry.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: He said that he couldn't say anything to his wife.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: He also talked about his wounds that were deep in his heart.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I was surprised to see how weak he was.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I couldn't tell anyone about the pain in his heart.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I prayed for him. After that, I saw his face getting brighter.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: It was a very precious experience for me to realize that the need for hospice, counseling, and chaplain was necessary.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: Koreans generally think that hospice is the cause of death.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: Why should I think about hospice?
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: I don't need to think about hospice.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: That's how I approach it. When I look at the systems I learned from the patients I met in the U.S. hospice,
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: in the U.S. society, this system of hospice is very cultured.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: When the end stage approaches, hospice becomes natural.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: Through that, they receive services and find ways to end their lives.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: In the Korean society, even in the U.S. society, hospice is a social system where we can think of death
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: as if we are being welcomed by the world.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: It is a social system. It is also a social benefit.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: Many people think that hospice is difficult, and it doesn't really matter to them.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: Also, don't be afraid of hospice.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: It is a system that helps the society to end my life
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: in a positive way.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: If you need to tap into hospice,
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: you can ask and receive counseling.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.

entries.text: If you decide, it will be great.
entries.notes: Auto-grouped to avoid one-word rolling fragments; requires Korean-English review.
